# hm

[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

Natural language shell commands. Explain what you want to execute and it generates the right command and runs it.

<p align="center">
  <img src="demo.gif" alt="hm demo" width="600">
</p>

`hm` sends your prompt to Claude, gets back a shell command, and runs it.

Requires an Anthropic API key. Works on macOS and Linux.

## Disclaimer

**Commands are generated by an LLM. You are responsible for what runs on your machine.**

`hm` generates and executes real shell commands. A bad prompt or a wrong guess from the model can delete files, overwrite data, or break your system. Do not use this tool if you are not comfortable reading and understanding shell commands.

Safety measures are in place but they are not foolproof:

- Commands flagged as dangerous (rm, sudo, force-push, etc.) require double confirmation before execution.
- Agent mode requires your approval before running any command.
- Dry mode (`.`) copies to clipboard without executing.

**In agent mode**, `hm` can read files on your system and send their contents to the Anthropic API. Shell history is also sent for context. Do not use agent or doctor mode in directories containing secrets you don't want sent to a third-party API.

## Install

```bash
brew install arndtvoges/hm/hm
```

Or build from source ([Bun](https://bun.sh) required):

```bash
git clone https://github.com/arndtvoges/hm.git
cd hm
bun install
bun run build
cp hm /usr/local/bin/hm
```

## Setup

On first run, `hm` will ask for your Anthropic API key and store it in your system keyring (macOS Keychain or Linux secret-tool).

You can also set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` as an environment variable instead.

## Usage

```bash
hm list all files including hidden ones
hm show disk usage sorted by size
hm kill whatever is running on port 3000
```

### Dry mode

Copies the command to clipboard instead of running it:

```bash
hm . compress this directory into a tar.gz
```

### Doctor mode

Run `hm` with no arguments to diagnose the last terminal error. It captures your terminal scrollback and shell history to figure out what went wrong and suggest a fix.

```bash
hm
```

Supports tmux, iTerm2, and Terminal.app for scrollback capture. Falls back to re-running the last command if scrollback isn't available.

### Agent mode

Use `hm ..` to start an interactive debugging agent. It can read files, search your codebase, and run commands (with your confirmation) to iteratively diagnose and fix issues.

```bash
hm .. why is my docker build failing
hm .. debug the test failures in src/
```

### Other

Reset your API key:

```bash
hm --reset-key
```

## Platform support

| Feature | macOS | Linux |
|---------|-------|-------|
| API key storage | Keychain (`security`) | libsecret (`secret-tool`) |
| Clipboard | `pbcopy` | `xclip` / `xsel` |
| Scrollback capture | tmux, Ghostty 1.3+, iTerm2, Terminal.app | tmux |
| Shell history | zsh, bash, fish | zsh, bash, fish |

WSL works via Linux support. Native Windows is not supported.

## Development

Requires [Bun](https://bun.sh) >= 1.1.0.

```bash
bun install
bun run src/cli.ts "your prompt here"
```

Build standalone binary:

```bash
bun run build
```
